items.title: NT fuel retailers forced to reveal full costs as 77yo law revived

children.content: The Northern Territory government has triggered a decades old law in an attempt to combat fuel price gouging at the bowser.

gallery.altText: A person paying for fuel at petrol station

thumbnail.altText: A person paying for fuel at petrol station

items.title: 19yo charged with murder after fatal stabbing in Katherine 'fist fight'

children.content: Police have charged a 19-year-old man with murder after he allegedly stabbed two other teenagers in Katherine on Sunday, leaving one dead and the other with critical injuries.

image.altText: Two police vehicles on street road, crime scene tape in front of cars, dark night, flashing lights on one car.

items.title: Darwin records second Indigenous death in custody within a week

children.content: In less than a week, another Indigenous man in his 20s has died in custody in Darwin. The incidents were not related, but the cause of death for both men remains unknown.

image.altText: An aerial photo of a correctional facility.

items.title: The ABC rode along with paramedics to examine the NT service's under-resourcing

children.content: Warren Purse, a Territory paramedic with over 20 years' experience, is assaulted on the job about three or four times a year.

image.altText: Mid-shot, white man, paramedic uniform, leaning against ambulance, arms crossed, gray hair serious expression.

articles.title: St John NT warns 'extraordinary' weekend measures 'absolutely not' safe

children.content: Northern Territory paramedics pushed "well and truly beyond our limits" at the weekend say the "extraordinary" measures taken in response are unsustainable and "absolutely not" safe.

image.altText: Female paramedic looks ahead with serious face

articles.title: Darwin's ambulance service fails overnight as 61 Triple Zero calls abandoned

children.content: Darwin's ambulance service has reached a "critical failure point" as 42 per cent of Triple Zero calls were abandoned overnight.

image.altText: Triple Zero emergency sign on St John NT ambulance

gallery.altText: Mid-shot, white man, paramedic uniform, leaning against ambulance, arms crossed, gray hair serious expression.

thumbnail.altText: Mid-shot, white man, paramedic uniform, leaning against ambulance, arms crossed, gray hair serious expression.

items.title: Fish prices to rise as fuel costs escalate, commercial industry warns

children.content: Farmers, fishers and business leaders are appealing to the federal and state governments to provide targeted relief to help them deal with massive fuel price increases caused by the Iran war, and the looming prospect of shortages.

image.altText: Two men in light coloured caps, one in a checked shirt, the other in a blue shirt stand in front of a bed of colourful flowers

items.title: Convenience stores selling illegal tobacco in Darwin spark organised crime fears

children.content: Convenience stores selling cheap, illegally packaged cigarettes have been popping up across Darwin and businesses are worried that organised crime and gang violence are next.

image.altText: Convenience store with red and black signage

items.title: A minute before Trump's wild Iran claim, oil traders made 'abnormal' moves

children.content: Global stock markets, which for the past month have been remarkably restrained as the Middle East crisis unfolded, have begun to wobble.

articles.title: How to tell this missile image is real

children.content: Huge amounts of false images and video circulating about the conflict in Iran are often making it difficult to distinguish genuine news from manipulated fakery, but this one is real.

image.altText: boy climbs on missile potruding from ground

articles.title: Trump threatens to 'obliterate' Kharg Island if Iran refuses deal to end war

children.content: As thousands more US troops arrive in the Middle East, Donald Trump threatens to wipe out Iran's energy plants and oil wells if it does not reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

image.altText: Donald Trump standing in a doorway aboard Air Force One.

articles.title: Israel passes death penalty for Palestinians convicted of lethal attacks

children.content: The law, long-sought by Israel's far-right politicians, makes death by hanging the default punishment for West Bank Palestinians convicted of killing Israelis.

image.altText: Benjamin Netanyahu looking stern at a press conference.

gallery.altText: A satellite view of the Strait of Hormuz.

thumbnail.altText: A satellite view of the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Fair Work Commission abolishes junior pay rates for young adults

children.content: The Fair Work Commission has issued its decision to abolish junior pay rates for young adult employees in the retail, fast food, and pharmacy sectors while maintaining them for minors.

image.altText: A person holds a turquoise coffee mug underneath the spout of an espresso machine

items.title: Coalition grills PM over fuel crisis during Question Time

children.content: Anthony Albanese faces a grilling from MPs as energy minister provides fuel availability update.
Follow live.

image.altText: Albanese speaks in the Lower House

items.title: What we know about where Dezi Freeman made his last stand

children.content: The rural property where police finally caught up with the fugitive gunman was a good place to disappear.

image.altText: Forensics investigating.

articles.title: Freeman death stirs shock, sadness, anger in scarred Porepunkah

children.content: Seven months after a gunman killed two police officers on a nearby property, the tiny town woke to news Dezi Freeman was dead.

image.altText: A man in a dark jacket and pants holding a phone to his ear walks past a brick building that says "Punkah Pantry".

articles.title: From Porepunkah to Thologolong: The long search for Dezi Freeman

children.content: When the parents of Senior Constable Vadim de Waart-Hottart flew to Melbourne for a police service, little did they know their son's killer would emerge just days later.

image.altText: An image showing a middle-aged man staring down the barrel of the camera.

articles.title: Police continue hunt for anyone who assisted fugitive Dezi Freeman

children.content: Police say they will come down hard on anyone who helped Dezi Freeman, who was shot dead by police yesterday after a massive seven-month manhunt.

image.altText: A screen grab of a man looking seriously at a the camera.

items.title: Telstra claims new coverage maps will 'make it much harder' for customers

children.content: ACMA has introduced new rules for standardised mobile coverage maps that will see mobile signals below -115 dBm labelled as "no coverage".

image.altText: The orange and white telstra sign is in focus in the background as people walk past in a blur.

items.title: Jackie O claiming 'at least' $82m in legal action against ARN Media

children.content: Former Sydney radio broadcaster Jackie "O" Henderson has filed legal action against employer ARN Media, following the collapse of the hit Kyle and Jackie O Show.

image.altText: A selfie of Sydney radio hosts Kyle Sandilands and Jackie O.

items.title: 'I'm sick of it': One in two Australians priced out of healthcare system

children.content: One in two Australians missed out on health care they needed last year mainly because they could not afford it, according to a new report by the peak healthcare consumers group.

articles.title: It's $2.5m a dose and it's giving some children another chance at life

children.content: Children born with a rare neuromuscular disorder are being given another chance in life thanks to a new treatment experts are calling a "game-changer".

image.altText: A young child being held up by her father

articles.title: Vaping likely to cause cancer, new Australian review of evidence finds

children.content: Nicotine-based vapes are likely to cause lung and oral cancer, according to a new review of evidence. Experts say there needs to be tougher enforcement of vape laws in Australia.

image.altText: young person with smoke coming out of their mouth after vaping

gallery.altText: A mum, a dad and their three children smile with forest behind them

thumbnail.altText: A mum, a dad and their three children smile with forest behind them

items.title: Consumer confidence hits 53-year low, ASX up, AUD flat

children.content: Consumer confidence across Australia has hit its worst level since tracking began in 1973, according to the latest ANZ-Roy Morgan survey, but the ASX 200 has bounced back to rise by almost 1 per cent. Follow the day's events on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: ASX

items.title: IOC's proposed gender test not fit for purpose, says man who discovered it

children.content: The scientist who discovered the SRY gene test that the International Olympic Committee is now relying on to determine athletes' eligibility for women's events says it should not be used in such a fashion.

image.altText: Olympic rings on a rainbow background.

items.title: Tim Rogers is an Aussie rock legend — that doesn't impress the bank

children.content: The frontman of ARIA Hall Of Famers You Am I on work, family, and why Hourly, Daily is still special to him.

items.title: What happens if your house is underinsured?

children.content: If your home is underinsured, it means your insurance policy won't fully cover the cost of rebuilding, repairing or replacing it.

image.altText: Grandfather and grandson walking to a yellow weatherboard home across a green lawn

items.title: Transphobia nearly killed Kaz. Now he just wants to be 'me'

children.content: This WA teenager was depressed, ill and missing out on school. He says it was part of the toll transphobia took on him.

image.altText: A boy stands in front of ocean scenery.

items.title: Supermarket chicken could get cheaper due to grain breakthrough

items.title: Cobram Estate praises America's 'grower-first mentality' after $260m deal

children.content: Australia's largest olive oil producer has completed its purchase of a massive olive grove in California, giving it about 70 per cent of the state's production.

items.title: The 10 pivotal events that led to the downfall of Essendon and Carlton

items.title: Businesses report increase in abuse from customers as fuel crisis worsens

items.title: Iran's 'safe passage' signals a new global divide

children.content: China is now the world's largest crude oil importer, and Iran — though a discounted and often opaque supplier — remains a consistent part of that supply mix.

items.title: How one man's stolen identity was used to run a cult

items.title: Black-robed lawyers and 'killer' beef at Kimberley court

items.title: Five social media companies investigated over response to teen ban

items.title: 'You are just an immigrant': Millions in unpaid wages, modern slavery uncovered

items.title: Céline Dion to perform again after years-long health-related hiatus
